candidate_fixture: Synthetic two-paper replication fixture: one ML-method paper capsule and one computational-science paper capsule, each with contribution decomposition, rubric tree, allowed public inputs, scratch repo scaffold, experiment DAG, metric scripts, artifact hashes, grader report, cost/runtime budget, ablation diff, failure taxonomy, and cold rerun receipt. Negative cases reject original-author code reuse when forbidden, hidden rubric leakage, report-only success, benchmark performance claims, private paper/data bodies, unbounded compute search, and final-answer-only grading.
failure_mode_prevented: A public research-agent demo claims to replicate a paper while hiding rubric coverage, artifact lineage, failed subtasks, compute budget, grader disagreement, original-code reuse, or unreplayable experiment state.
notes: Added after external attention review of PaperBench and CORE-Bench. This is a synthetic replication-receipt contract folded under existing proof/evidence, mission, and observability organs; it is not a PaperBench score, live paper replication claim, publication claim, or use of private source material.
one_line: A public-safe research-agent fixture replays paper-to-code replication through a paper capsule, author/rubric hierarchy, artifact manifest, experiment DAG, score rubric, grader trace, cost budget, and cold rerun.
title: Research replication rubric-artifact replay compound
why_load_bearing: Research-replication agents are one of the most impressive public AI surfaces because the task crosses reading, implementation, experiment execution, and judgment. The credible mechanism is not a polished report or benchmark score; it is a replayable rubric-to-artifact harness that shows exactly what was reproduced, what failed, what evidence was graded, and what was kept out of scope.
